mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPI) over a 6-month period indicates rising inflation expectations, which can lead to higher interest rates and decreased consumer spending, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% decrease in the Unemployment Rate over a 3-month period indicates a tightening labor market, which can lead to higher wages and increased consumer spending, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening labor markets are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the Industrial Production Index over a 6-month period indicates increased economic activity, which can lead to higher demand for raw materials and increased production costs, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% increase in the federal funds rate over a 3-month period indicates a tightening monetary cycle, which can lead to higher interest rates and decreased consumer spending, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% decrease in the RSAFS (Retail Sales) over a 3-month period indicates decreased consumer spending, which can lead to lower demand for goods and services, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1% increase in the PAYEMS (Nonfarm Payroll Employment) over a 3-month period indicates increased labor market activity, which can lead to higher wages and increased consumer spending, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in tightening labor markets are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2% increase in the HOUST (Housing Starts) over a 6-month period indicates increased housing market activity, which can lead to higher demand for raw materials and increased production costs, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.
